symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive exertional dyspnoea with reduced exercise tolerance (subacute-on-chronic)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional dyspnoea is the commonest presenting symptom of pulmonary hypertension but is non-specific in isolation.
Also produces exertional breathlessness — distinguished by orthopnoea/PND/crackles, which are absent here.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional syncope/pre-syncope in suspected PH/RV strain signals inability to augment cardiac output on exertion — a high-risk feature and sudden-death warning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral peripheral oedema and abdominal fullness (systemic venous congestion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral oedema plus abdominal fullness reflects right heart failure / systemic venous congestion.
Oedema occurs in biventricular failure too; on its own it does not localise to the right heart.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low cardiac output causes fatigue but is highly non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of orthopnoea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea and chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent orthopnoea/PND makes left-sided failure with pulmonary oedema less likely and shifts weight toward a right-sided process.
No chest pain reduces (but over telehealth cannot exclude) an acute coronary cause of the presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting hypoxia SpO2 92% on room air, respiratory rate 22, heart rate 95

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Resting hypoxia and tachypnoea indicate impaired gas exchange / acute-on-chronic decompensation — clinician-measured, not obtainable in this chat as the patient has no home device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left parasternal RV heave, loud/split P2, right-sided S3 gallop

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable RV heave with a loud P2 and right-sided S3 is a classic examination signature of a pressure-loaded, failing right ventricle — but requires palpation and auscultation, sealed on the answer-key side.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated JVP (7 cm above sternal angle) with prominent 'a' wave; tender hepatomegaly; 2+ bilateral pitting oedema; clear lungs

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated JVP, tender hepatomegaly and graded pitting oedema with CLEAR lungs localise congestion to the right heart, not the pulmonary circulation — clinician examination findings, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transthoracic echocardiogram (PA pressure estimate, RV function) and BNP

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated estimated pulmonary artery systolic pressure with a dilated, hypokinetic right ventricle; raised BNP
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pulmonary hypertension and RV failure remain unconfirmed and severity unstaged; the dangerous mimics are not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiography is the first-line non-invasive test to estimate PA pressures and assess RV function; right heart catheterisation is the definitive confirmatory test.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Right heart failure with exertional pre-syncope
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional pre-syncope combined with signs of systemic venous congestion and a pressure-loaded right ventricle is a high-risk constellation demanding urgent assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line non-invasive test to estimate pulmonary artery pressure and assess right ventricular function.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: BNP / NT-proBNP and basic metabolic panel
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support and stage heart failure and assess renal function / electrolytes before any diuretic decision (specialist/GP-led, not remote).
Assess for right heart strain / right axis deviation and exclude alternative causes as part of the same-day in-person work-up.
Definitive haemodynamic confirmation of pulmonary hypertension — arranged by the specialist service, not first-line and never via telehealth.
